---
title: "Software"
toc: true
toc-title: ""
---

I develop and maintain open-source R packages across statistical graphics, algebraic statistics, Bayesian methods, and more. Source code is on [GitHub](https://github.com/dkahle).

## Statistical Graphics

### [ggmap](https://github.com/dkahle/ggmap) <small class="text-muted">with Hadley Wickham</small>

Spatial visualization with ggplot2. Downloads map tiles from Google Maps, OpenStreetMap, and Stamen Maps and renders them as ggplot2 objects. One of the most widely used R packages for spatial data visualization.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/ggmap) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=ggmap) · 7.5M downloads

---

### [ggdensity](https://jamesotto852.github.io/ggdensity/) <small class="text-muted">with James Otto</small>

Interpretable bivariate density visualization for ggplot2 using highest density regions (HDRs). HDRs convey where the data actually are, rather than the density value itself, making them more natural to communicate.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/jamesotto852/ggdensity) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=ggdensity) [[Docs]](https://jamesotto852.github.io/ggdensity/) · 38K downloads

---

### [ggvfields](https://dustysturner.com/ggvfields/) <small class="text-muted">with Dusty Turner and Rodney Sturdivant</small>

Vector field visualization for ggplot2. Provides geoms for visualizing vector-valued functions and fields, with applications in physics, fluid dynamics, and statistical modeling.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dustysturner/ggvfields) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=ggvfields) [[Docs]](https://dustysturner.com/ggvfields/) · 2K downloads

---

### [ggfunction](https://github.com/dkahle/ggfunction) <small class="text-muted">with Dusty Turner, James Otto, and Rodney Sturdivant · in development</small>

Mathematical functions and probability distributions, along with estimates, in ggplot2.

[[GitHub]](https://dustysturner.com/ggfunction)

---

### [ggclassify](https://github.com/dkahle/ggclassify) <small class="text-muted">with Nathaniel Morgan and Michael Gallaugher · in development</small>

Visualizing classifiers in R. Extends ggplot2 with tools for displaying decision boundaries and classification regions from statistical and machine learning models.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/ggclassify)

---

### [biroot](https://github.com/dkahle/biroot) <small class="text-muted">with Nathaniel Morgan and Michael Gallaugher · in development</small>

Tools for computing and visualizing bivariate level sets in R.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/biroot)

---

## Algebraic Statistics

### [mpoly](https://github.com/dkahle/mpoly)

Symbolic computation with multivariate polynomials in R. Provides a full framework for polynomial arithmetic, calculus, evaluation, and manipulation in multiple variables.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/mpoly) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=mpoly) · 113K downloads

---

### [vnorm](https://github.com/sonish13/vnorm) <small class="text-muted">with Sonish Lamsal · in development</small>

The variety normal distribution in R. Provides tools for working with probability distributions supported on algebraic varieties.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/vnorm)

---

### [m2r](https://github.com/coneill-math/m2r) <small class="text-muted">with Christopher O'Neill and Jeff Sommars</small>

An interface from R to Macaulay2, a computer algebra system for algebraic geometry and commutative algebra. Enables polynomial ring computations from within R.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/coneill-math/m2r) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=m2r) · 32K downloads

---



### [algstat](https://github.com/dkahle/algstat) <small class="text-muted">with Luis Garcia-Puente and Ruriko Yoshida</small>

Algebraic statistics in R. Provides tools for exact inference in discrete exponential family models and Markov chain Monte Carlo methods for contingency tables.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/algstat) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=algstat)

---

### [latte](https://github.com/dkahle/latte) <small class="text-muted">with Luis Garcia-Puente and Ruriko Yoshida</small>

An R interface to LattE Macchiato and 4ti2 for counting lattice points in polytopes and solving integer programming problems. Used for exact inference in log-linear models.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/latte) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=latte)

---

### [bertini](https://github.com/dkahle/bertini)

An R interface to Bertini, a software package for numerical algebraic geometry. Enables solving polynomial systems and computing algebraic varieties from R.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/bertini)

---

### [tropical](https://github.com/dkahle/tropical) <small class="text-muted">with Peixin Gao, Grant Innerst, Dohyun Kim, Ruriko Yoshida, and Xi Zhang</small>

Tropical geometry in R. Provides tools for computing and visualizing tropical algebraic objects.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/tropical)

---

## Probability Distributions

### [invgamma](https://github.com/dkahle/invgamma) <small class="text-muted">with James Stamey</small>

The inverse gamma distribution in R: density, distribution, quantile, and random generation functions following base R conventions.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/invgamma) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=invgamma) · 405K downloads

---

### [chi](https://github.com/dkahle/chi)

The chi distribution (not chi-squared) in R: density, distribution, quantile, and random generation functions.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/chi) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=chi) · 43K downloads

---

### [dirchlet](https://github.com/dkahle/dirchlet)

The Dirichlet distribution in R: density, random generation, and related functions following base R conventions.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/dirchlet)

---

### [betalu](https://github.com/dkahle/betalu)

The beta distribution with support [l, u] in R, generalizing the standard beta distribution to arbitrary bounded intervals.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/betalu)

---

## Bayesian Statistics

### [unmconf](https://github.com/dkahle/unmconf) <small class="text-muted">with Ryan Hebdon, James Stamey, and Xiao Zhang</small>

Bayesian regression with unmeasured confounders. Provides tools for fitting models that account for unmeasured confounding via informative priors.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/unmconf) · 5K downloads

---

### [bayesRates](https://github.com/dkahle/bayesRates) <small class="text-muted">with James Stamey and Ryan Sides</small>

Two-sample tests and sample size determination from a Bayesian perspective for Poisson and binomial models.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/bayesRates)

---

## Applied Statistics

### [TITAN2](https://cran.r-project.org/package=TITAN2) <small class="text-muted">with Matthew Baker and Ryan King</small>

Threshold Indicator Taxa Analysis. Identifies community change points along environmental gradients using indicator species analysis and bootstrapping.

[[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=TITAN2) · 43K downloads

---

### [poisDoubleSamp](https://github.com/dkahle/poisDoubleSamp) <small class="text-muted">with Philip Young and Dean Young</small>

Confidence intervals for the ratio of two Poisson rates under one-way differential misclassification using double sampling.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/poisDoubleSamp) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=poisDoubleSamp) · 33K downloads

---


## Documentation & Tools

### [tldrPages](https://github.com/jamesotto852/tldrPages) <small class="text-muted">with James Otto</small>

Short-form documentation in the R console. Provides compact, scannable help pages for R functions as an alternative to full `?` documentation.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/jamesotto852/tldrPages)

---

### [tldrDocs](https://github.com/jamesotto852/tldrDocs) <small class="text-muted">with James Otto</small>

tldrPages documentation for base R functions, providing the content layer for the tldrPages system.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/jamesotto852/tldrDocs) [[CRAN]](https://cran.r-project.org/package=tldrDocs)

---

### [registers](https://github.com/dkahle/registers) <small class="text-muted">with James Otto</small>

Registers for R. Tools for working with register-based data structures.

[[GitHub]](https://github.com/dkahle/registers)
